text:
*NOTE: Yes I know your comment is old.*

Different people have different opinions, that's how he (and I) can like the game.  Personally, I disagree with your points, as I found the characters and plot interesting (Though confusing at some points.) and the game play was easy to follow and get addicted to.  Though XIII-2 was by far the better game.

It all just boils down to personal opinion and taste.  You don't like it, and that's fine.

text: I think everyone can appreciate some of the things they did in FFXIII battle system, graphics, and development of personalities of hero characters was all a step in the right direction. I just felt the game had alot of potential that they just didn't put in enough time and effort to fix to make it as good as some of the older ones. Villain wasn't developed well, All the sidequests were crammed in, one area No Exploration or revisiting old areas allowed, and ending didn't really resolve anything.

text:
I love ff7,8,9,10, and 10-2. FF12 was ok, not enough interaction or exploration of world, and the story is basically finding rocks, finding a bigger rock and destroying the rocks. I had more interest in the sidequests in ff12 than the main one, I should have felt torn between them but the main didn't interest me at all. FF13 I didn't bother finishing, beautiful game but very linear and too boring.

Dragon age/ fallout/ mass effect should be given as an example of good rpgs of the 21st century.

text:
@7255202 You are absolutely on the spot with those observations. I couldn't agree more. 

The graphics were fantastic. The exploration..not so much. In fact, not at all. 

There were virtually no NPC's in the game. Nothing was hidden, and so there was nothing to be found. 

That's basically what I experienced with FFXIII.
